titleStmt.title: SII 23.158: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 158. (A.R. No. 158 of 1907.) Pēraiyūr, Tirumayam Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District Nāganāthasvāmi temple—on the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine Dēvarāya II: Śaka 1343

summary.p: This is dated in Śaka 1343, Plava, Mārgaḻi, 3, śu. 6, Sunday, Aviṭṭam (A.D. 1421, November 30) in the reign of Dēvarāya Uḍaiyar, son of Ariyarāya (Harihara). It stops with the mention of Tirumēṉiyaḻagiyār Vi[ḍai-nāvalaṉ]dēvar Śeṇbagarāyaṉ alias Vijayālaya••••• of Sūraikkuḍi in Tennāṟṟuppōkku a division of Adaḷaiyūr-nāḍu of Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu, who was probably a local chief.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . mahāmaṇḍaliśvaraṉ Ariyarāyavibhāṭan bhāṣaikku tappuva rāyar kaṇṭaṉ muvarāyar kaṇṭaṉ kaṇṭanāṭu koṇṭu koṇṭanāṭu kuṭātān pūrvvadakṣiṇapaści
ma Uttarasamudrādhipati Ariyarāya kumāran devarāyauṭaiyaṟkuc cellā niṉṟa śakābdam 1000 3 100 4 10 3- ṉmel cellāninṟa pilavavaruṣam mārkaḻi 3 tiyati pūrvvapakṣattu ṣaṣṭiyum Ādityavāṟa
mum peṟṟa Aviṭṭattunāḷ keraḷaciṅka vaḷanāṭṭu Ataḷaiyūrnāṭṭu tennāṟṟuppokku cūraikkuṭi tirumeṉiyaḻakiyārāṉa viṭai nā valaṉ tevar ceṇpakarāyaṉāṉa vicaiyālaiStops here.
Digital edition of SII 23.158 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
